mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, which lowers borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor market conditions, which drive consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts (HOUST) reflects growing demand for residential construction, signaling economic expansion and boosting equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises > 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising industrial production (INDPRO) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong economy with robust manufacturing and labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO > 105 and UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal central bank intervention, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side activity, which supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises > 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and higher corporate costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a healthy economy with strong demand and controlled price pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS > 435k and CPIAUCSL < 2.8%.
